steps.title: Identify unit prices from the equation coefficients
steps.content:
The equation $26.4x + 26.7y = 691.2$ represents a cost relationship. It has the form:
$$(\text{cost per unit of mulch}) \cdot x + (\text{cost per unit of gravel}) \cdot y = \text{total cost}$$
Comparing with our equation, the coefficient of $x$ is the cost per cubic yard of mulch: $26.40.
The coefficient of $y$ is the cost per ton of gravel: $26.70.

steps.title: Find the price difference
steps.content:
Subtract the unit price of mulch from the unit price of gravel:
$$26.70 - 26.4 = 0.30$$
A ton of gravel cost Priya $0.30 more than a cubic yard of mulch.
